name: Scan Cone
summary: A rotating volumetric Cesium cone with optional smooth radius-and-length expansion and per-frame progress callbacks.
useCases: Drone detection and rotating search cone

center.description: WGS84 origin point. Optional height is supported.

color.description: CSS color used by the cone material.

radiusMeters.description: Static cone bottom radius in meters when expansion is omitted.

speed.description: Rotation and material animation speed multiplier.

showOrigin.description: Show a point marker at the cone origin.

expansion.description: Optional smooth expansion that grows the cone radius and length together.

cameraFollow.description: Opt in to one smart camera flight that frames the final cone and yields when the user takes camera control.

autoStart.description: Start expansion automatically when the effect is created. Set false to wait for restartExpansion().

onFrame.description: Called once per rendered expansion frame with status, progress, radiusMeters, lengthMeters, and elapsedMs. Keep the callback lightweight.

onComplete.description: Called once after the final completed frame with the exact final expansion state.

update.code:
cone.update({
  type: 'alarm',
  color: '#ff315a',
  speed: 2.6,
  heading: 45,
  showOrigin: false,
  expansion: {
    maxRadiusMeters: 4800,
    durationMs: 6000,
    cameraFollow: false,
    onFrame: (frame) => console.log(frame.radiusMeters),
  },
})

cone.restartExpansion()

notes: Pass an existing Cesium Viewer; the SDK does not create or own the viewer.
Without expansion, the effect keeps the existing static Cesium Entity path with CylinderGraphics and GeoScanConeMaterial.
Expansion uses one Cesium Primitive and updates its model matrix in place; it does not rebuild geometry every frame.
cameraFollow is opt-in and defaults to false. Pointer, wheel, or touch input yields camera ownership to the user without stopping expansion.
Changing maxRadiusMeters, durationMs, or lengthMeters through update() restarts the expansion run.
Call destroy() during page cleanup to remove the entity or primitive, data source, camera listeners, and render loop.
